strategy: Hit with Myth, Life, or Death. He takes +30% from all three. Skip Balance (42% resist).
Do not use Traps at all. Both forms Cleanse Ward your Traps, and the Shadow form also lays a team Shadow Trap. Stack blades instead.
Expect a random spell every third Round. Keep a Cleanse Charm ready for the Efreet (-90% Damage) or Loremaster (-20% Damage / -35% Accuracy) rolls.
Burst the Balance form down fast. Every fourth Round after summoning he Sacrifices one of his own Minions to heal 2,000.
When the Balance form dies he Polymorphs into a Shadow form and summons two Minions. Reload blades and repeat the blade-and-burst plan for phase two.
cheatNotes: Casting any Trap makes him Cleanse Ward all Traps off himself, so traps are wasted here. Build with blades instead.
Every third Round he casts one random spell from the list below, ranging from a summon to a hard-hitting attack.
This roll is a 4-pip Loremaster that hits 390-470 Balance and applies -20% Damage and -35% Accuracy charms on you. Cleanse them before your hit.
This roll is an Efreet dealing 895 Fire plus a -90% Damage charm on you. Clear that charm or your next attack does almost nothing.
When his Balance form dies he immediately Polymorphs into a Shadow form, so the fight has a second phase. Save resources for it.
On his first Round as the Shadow form he summons two random Minions you will need to clear.
In Shadow form, casting a Trap makes him Cleanse all your traps and then lay a Shadow Trap on your whole team. Again, avoid traps.
Every fourth Round after summoning he Sacrifices one of his own Minions to heal 2,000, so kill his Minions before he can eat them for health.
Clear all his Minions and he just resummons more next Round. Race the boss down rather than chasing minions forever.
